items.title: Nobel Laureate John Jumper Departs DeepMind for Anthropic
items.summary: Nobel laureate John Jumper, a key figure in AlphaFold's development, is reportedly leaving DeepMind to join rival AI research company Anthropic, signaling a significant talent shift in the industry.

items.title: OpenAI to Acquire Ona
items.summary: OpenAI has announced its acquisition of Ona, a company focused on data collection and analysis in challenging environments, likely to enhance OpenAI's data capabilities for AI model training and deployment.

items.title: DragMesh-2: Physically Plausible Dexterous Hand-Object Interaction with Articulated Objects
items.summary: Researchers introduce DragMesh-2, a new method enabling physically plausible and dexterous hand-object interactions with articulated objects, significantly advancing robotics and virtual reality applications.

items.title: Signal's Meredith Whittaker Warns Against Trusting AI Chatbots
items.summary: Meredith Whittaker of Signal cautions users to remember that AI chatbots are not personal friends, emphasizing the importance of privacy and critical evaluation of AI interactions.

items.title: Goose Offers Free Alternative to Claude Code's $200/Month Service
items.summary: A new service named Goose claims to offer the same functionality as Claude Code, which costs up to $200 a month, entirely for free, potentially disrupting the market for AI coding assistants.

items.title: OpenAI Launches New Academy Courses for AI Application in the Workplace
items.summary: OpenAI has introduced new Academy courses designed to equip individuals with the skills to apply AI effectively in various professional settings, catering to the evolving demands of the modern workforce.

items.title: FlowBender: Feedback-Aware Training for Self-Correcting Conditional Flows
items.summary: FlowBender introduces a novel feedback-aware training mechanism that enables conditional flows to self-correct, promising more robust and adaptable generative models.

items.title: JanusMesh: Fast and Zero-Shot 3D Visual Illusion Generation via Cross-Space Denoising
items.summary: JanusMesh presents a new technique for generating 3D visual illusions rapidly and without prior examples, utilizing cross-space denoising to create compelling visual effects.

items.title: ImageWAM: Do World Action Models Really Need Video Generation, or Just Image Editing?
items.summary: This research investigates whether World Action Models truly require complex video generation capabilities or if advanced image editing techniques suffice for their operational needs, potentially simplifying model design.

items.title: Current World Models Lack a Persistent State Core
items.summary: A new paper argues that current world models are missing a persistent state core, highlighting a fundamental limitation in their ability to maintain consistent understanding and memory over time.

items.title: FAPO: Fully Autonomous Prompt Optimization of Multi-Step LLM Pipelines
items.summary: FAPO introduces a method for fully autonomous prompt optimization within multi-step LLM pipelines, aiming to enhance the efficiency and performance of complex AI workflows.

items.title: ENPIRE: Agentic Robot Policy Self-Improvement in the Real World
items.summary: ENPIRE proposes a framework for agentic robot policy self-improvement directly in real-world environments, paving the way for more autonomous and adaptable robotic systems.

items.title: MaineCoon: Pursuing A Real-Time Audio-Visual Social World Model
items.summary: MaineCoon is a research initiative focused on developing a real-time audio-visual social world model, aiming to create AI systems that can understand and interact with complex social environments.

items.title: AI Memory Should Be a Product State, Not a Prompt Trick
items.summary: This article argues that AI memory should be integrated as a fundamental product state rather than relying on prompt engineering tricks, advocating for more robust and reliable AI interactions.

items.title: Hidden Anchors in Multi-Agent LLM Deliberation
items.summary: New research explores the concept of 'hidden anchors' that influence decision-making in multi-agent LLM deliberation, shedding light on the underlying mechanisms of collective AI reasoning.
